{"step_by_step_thinking": "Terfenadine is an antihistamine commonly used to treat allergic rhinitis. However, it has a known interaction with certain antibiotics, particularly erythromycin, which can cause QT interval prolongation and lead to cardiac arrhythmias. Ampicillin, tetracycline, and co-trimoxazole do not have the same known interaction with terfenadine. Therefore, the antibiotic that could have caused the cardiac arrhythmias is erythromycin.", "answer_choice": "C"}
